Complementary Results

About

Description

Oscar-Claude Monet was a French painter and founder of Impressionism who is seen as a key precursor to modernism, especially in his attempts to paint nature as he perceived it. Wikipedia
Born: November 14, 1840, Rue Laffitte, Paris, France
Died: December 5, 1926 (age 86 years), Giverny, France
Periods: Impressionism, Modern art
Spouse: Alice Hoschedé (m. 1892–1911), Camille Doncieux (m. 1870–1879)
Influenced by: Pierre-Auguste Renoir, Camille Pissarro · See more
Children: Michel Monet, Jean Monet
Full name: Oscar-Claude Monet
